title: Democritus And Heraclitus
content:
Democritus, dear droll, revisit earth,
And with our follies glut thy heighten'd mirth:
Sad Heraclitus, serious wretch, return,
In louder grief our greater crimes to mourn,
Between you both I unconcern'd stand by;
Hurt can I lauh? and honest need I cry?
meaning:
Matthew Prior's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Democritus And Heraclitus"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
